subject: [PATCH 0/6 v3] mm/memcontrol, page_counter: move stock from mem_cgroup to page_counter

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Raised the issue of synchronous draining work and its potential impact on system behavior, specifically the WARN_ON due to flushing a !wq_mem_reclaim wq. Proposed three options: trust that it's OK, use memcg_wq marked WQ_MEM_RECLAIM, or go back to asynchronous.

2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch series moves the per-cpu stock from mem_cgroup to page_counter, consolidating stock limit checking and page_counter limit checking into a single function. This change eliminates the 7-memcg-per-cpu slot limit, random evictions (drain & refill), and slot traversal. The resulting code in memcg is also easier to follow, as caching becomes transparent from memcg's perspective and managed entirely within page_counter.
